svg.title: How often lawyer-coders contribute to repos they don’t own or work for
svg.desc: A wedge-shaped beeswarm. Each dot is one lawyer-coder, coloured by cohort and sized by total filing rate. Y-axis: PRs they filed that got merged into a repo they don’t own and aren’t affiliated with, per year on GitHub (log scaled, capped at 20). Affiliated orgs are discounted for 14 outliers — own employers, board roles, and founded projects. A dense base carpet of mostly-blue dots (LegalQuants) sits along the bottom; a thinner stack of mostly-green dots (LawyersOnGitHub) climbs the wedge; amber active veterans cluster near the apex but no longer at outlier heights once their own-org contributions are removed.
svg.text: How often lawyer-coders contribute to repos they don’t own or work for
Each dot is one lawyer-coder. Vertical position = PRs that got merged into a repo they don’t own and aren’t affiliated with, per year on GitHub.
Dot size = total PRs filed per year. Open marker = under 2 years on GitHub. Y-axis log scaled.
Affiliated orgs (own employer, board roles, founded projects) excluded for 14 outliers.

g.text: External merged PRs per year (excl. affiliated orgs)
